query: PostgreSQL MongoDB DynamoDB analytics ecosystem tools real-time analytics support
answer: MongoDB and ClickHouse support real-time analytics, while DynamoDB uses Tinybird for real-time data processing. ClickHouse excels in low-latency, high-concurrency real-time analytics.

results.title: Real-time Data Analytics On MongoDB Atlas | MongoDB
results.snippet: With MongoDB, businesses can analyze any data in place and deliver insights in real time. By combining data from real-time events with historic and reference datasets, organizations can optimize queries to quickly deliver actionable results. ## Building real-time applications with MongoDB’s modern data platform. A diagram showcasing the various data sources used to create real time applications. Real-time data reflects what is happening now. With MongoDB, real-time analytics can be derived from multiple data sources — from basic aggregations to machine learning and AI — and stored separately. * ACID-compliant databases to ensure the ability to react to new data in real time and maintain high data integrity while serving many concurrent queries. Configuring and developing real-time analytics with high productivity — meaning less time wasted mapping data tables or coding single-use data pipelines — means you’re making your data a competitive advantage. * Offers a variety of efficient options for delivering insights to data consumers in real time, including change streams, triggers, and GraphQL.

results.title: PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and DynamoDB: A Quick Comparison
results.snippet: The article covers core data models, querying capabilities, similarities and differences, use cases, serverless and scaling considerations, pricing, performance, availability, developer experience, ecosystem, common pitfalls, and anti-patterns. Instead of storing data in tables and rows as in traditional relational databases, MongoDB uses collections and documents, where each document is a JSON-like object (BSON) that can have varying structures. This structure supports complex queries, joins, and transactional integrity, making it ideal for applications requiring consistency and structured data. DynamoDB utilizes a key-value and document data model optimized for high-throughput and scalability. It helps to quickly identify which database might be the best fit for specific use cases based on factors like data model, query capabilities, transactions, indexing, replication, consistency, scaling, cloud offerings, and common pitfalls. * **Global Distribution:** Global Tables replicate data across multiple AWS regions with multi-active writes, providing low-latency access and disaster recovery. You provision (or use on-demand) read and write throughput, and DynamoDB automatically partitions data based on your primary key.

results.title: Combining MongoDB, PeerDB, and ClickHouse for real-time analytics | Franck Pachot posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.snippet: • High-performance initial loads + real-time Change Streams • Native JSON data type support: Preserve nested docs, arrays, embedded objects • Enterprise-grade monitoring and alerting • Powered by open-source PeerDB — no vendor lock-in 📝 Sign up for the Private Preview and help shape the future of document analytics in ClickHouse: https://lnkd.in/dzBCJPg8. 🚀 Multi-Tenant Analytics Platform using MongoDB In a fast-scaling SaaS analytics platform, every customer (tenant) expects isolated data, fast insights, and real-time dashboards — without paying for a dedicated database cluster. By designing a shared collection with a tenantID field and sharding on tenantID, we can serve thousands of customers from a single cluster — while maintaining: 🔒 Data Isolation → Queries always scoped by tenantID ⚡ Performance → Tenant-level compound indexes {tenantID, timestamp} for real-time analytics 📊 Scalability → Shards auto-balance tenants across nodes as load increases 🧩 Flexibility → Each tenant can store custom metadata, dashboards, or schema extensions Using MongoDB Aggregation Pipelines and Change Streams, the platform delivers real-time analytics per tenant — all within milliseconds — without duplicating infrastructure.

results.title: How to choose a database for real-time analytics in 2026 - ClickHouse
results.snippet: * If you need **sub-second p95 analytical queries on fresh data** with **high concurrency** (e.g., 50–100+ concurrent users/QPS), use a real-time OLAP database —cloud DWHs and Postgres typically hit latency/cost/concurrency limits. This guide is for Data Architects, Platform Engineers, and CTOs evaluating backends for user-facing analytics, high-throughput telemetry, or real-time decisioning. A recent benchmark of all five major cloud data warehouses at 1B, 10B, and 100B rows shows this effect clearly: as data grows, every traditional DWH drifts toward "Slow & High-Cost," with cost-performance gaps widening to 23× or more compared to purpose-built real-time engines. * **Choose real-time OLAP (ClickHouse) when:** Your application is user-facing (low latency), or you have high query volume (high concurrency). Real-time OLAP wins when you have high volume (TB/PB), high concurrency (>50 concurrent queries), and a low latency requirement (<1s p95). If you need **sub-second p95 latency** on **fresh data** with **high concurrency**, choose a **real-time OLAP database** (e.g., ClickHouse).
